regional.d: Of, or pertaining to, a specific region or district.
regional.e: Regional languages are an important part of Indian culture.

register.d: To place one's name, or have one's name placed in a register.

regret.d: To feel sorry about (a thing that has or has not happened), afterthink: to wish that a thing had not happened, that something else had happened instead.

regulation.d: In conformity with applicable rules and regulations.
regulation.e: New traffic regulations have been introduced to improve road safety.

relatively.d: Proportionally, in relation to some larger scale thing.
relatively.e: He measured his success relatively, that is, competitively.

relevant.d: Directly related, connected, or pertinent to a topic.
relevant.e: His mother provided some relevant background information concerning his medical condition.

relief.d: The removal of stress or discomfort.
relief.e: I sighed with relief when I found out that my daughter hadn't got lost, but was waiting for me at home.

rely.d: (with on or upon, formerly also with in) to trust; to have confidence in; to depend.

remark.d: An expression, in speech or writing, of something remarked or noticed; a mention of something

representative.e: Are you sure this paper is representative of your child's writing?

reputation.e: The restaurant has an excellent reputation for its South Indian food.

requirement.d: A necessity or prerequisite; something required or obligatory. Its adpositions are generally of in relation to who or what has given it, on in relation to whom or what it is given to, and for in relation to what is required.
requirement.e: There was a requirement of the government on citizens for paying taxes.

rescue.d: To save from any violence, danger or evil.
rescue.e: The well-trained team rescued everyone after the avalanche.

reserve.d: To keep in store for future or special use.
reserve.e: This cake is reserved for the guests!

resident.d: Dwelling, or having an abode, in a place for a continued length of time; residing on one's own estate.
resident.e: resident in the city or in the country

resist.d: To attempt to counter the actions or effects of.
resist.e: She could not resist buying the beautiful saree.

resolve.d: To make a firm decision to do something.
resolve.e: I resolve to finish this work before I go home.

resort.d: Recourse, refuge (something or someone turned to for safety).

retain.d: To employ by paying a retainer.
retain.e: The company works hard to retain its best employees.

reveal.d: A revelation; an uncovering of what was hidden.
reveal.e: The comedian had been telling us about his sleep being disturbed by noise. Then came the reveal: he was sleeping on a bed in a department store.

revolution.d: A political upheaval in a government or nation state characterized by great change.
revolution.e: The Green Revolution transformed agriculture in India.

reward.d: To give a reward to or for.
reward.e: Why are you rewarding that bad behaviour?

rhythm.d: The musical instruments which provide rhythm (mainly; not or less melody) in a musical ensemble.
rhythm.e: The Baroque term basso continuo is virtually equivalent to rhythm

rid.d: To convey, as by riding; to make or do by riding.
rid.e: How many races have you ridden this year?

root.d: To prepare, oversee, or otherwise cause the rooting of cuttings
root.e: We rooted some cuttings last summer.

rub.d: To rub something against (a second thing).
rub.e: I rubbed the glass with the cloth.

rubber.d: The rectangular pad on the pitcher's mound from which the pitcher must pitch.
rubber.e: Jones toes the rubber and then fires to the plate.

rural.d: Relating to the countryside or to agriculture.
rural.e: Many rural areas in India now have internet access.

rush.d: To flow or move forward rapidly or noisily.
rush.e: armies rush to battle;   waters rush down a precipice.

satellite.d: Satellite TV; reception of television broadcasts via services that utilize man-made satellite technology.
satellite.e: Do you have satellite at your house?

satisfied.d: To pay to the extent of what is claimed or due.

satisfy.d: To pay to the extent of what is claimed or due.

saving.d: Bringing back in returns or in receipts the sum expended; incurring no loss, though not gainful.
saving.e: The ship has made a saving voyage.

scale.e: Please scale that fish for dinner.

scheme.d: To plot, or contrive a plan.
scheme.e: The government launched a scheme to provide clean drinking water.

scream.e: He almost hit a pole, the way he came screaming down the hill.

secure.d: To get possession of; to make oneself secure of; to acquire certainly.

seek.d: To try to find; to look for; to search for.

select.d: To choose one or more elements of a set, especially a set of options.
select.e: He looked over the menu, and selected the roast beef.

selection.d: The process or act of selecting.
selection.e: The large number of good candidates made selection difficult.

self.d: Having its own or a single nature or character throughout, as in colour, composition, etc., without addition or change; of the same kind; unmixed.
self.e: a self bow: one made from a single piece of wood

senior.d: Higher in rank, dignity, or office.

sensitive.d: Meant to be concealed or kept secret.

sequence.e: Complete the listed tasks in sequence.

session.d: To hold or participate in a jam session with other musicians.
session.e: This court is now in session.

settle.d: To fix one's residence in a place; to establish a dwelling place, home, or colony. (Compare settle down.)
settle.e: the Saxons who settled in Britain

shade.d: To vary or approach something slightly, particularly in color.

shadow.d: That which looms as though a shadow.

shallow.d: A shallow portion of an otherwise deep body of water.
shallow.e: The ship ran aground in an unexpected shallow.

shame.e: I was shamed by the teacher's public disapproval.

shelter.e: During the rainstorm, we sheltered under a tree.

shock.d: To cause to be emotionally shocked, to cause (someone) to feel surprised and upset.

shocked.d: To cause to be emotionally shocked, to cause (someone) to feel surprised and upset.

shooting.d: An instance of shooting with a gun or other weapon.
shooting.e: Police are hunting the people who carried out the shootings last week.

shot.d: The heavy iron ball used for the shot put.
shot.e: The shot flew twenty metres, and nearly landed on the judge's foot.

significant.d: Having a noticeable or major effect; notable.
significant.e: That was a significant step in the right direction.

significantly.d: In a significant manner or to a significant extent.
significantly.e: Her English has improved significantly since she started practising.

silence.e: Can you silence the crowd, so we can start the show?

silk.d: A fine fiber excreted by the silkworm or other arthropod (such as a spider).
silk.e: The thread made of silk was barely visible.

sincere.d: Genuine; meaning what one says or does; heartfelt.
sincere.e: I believe he is sincere in his offer to help.

slave.d: To place a device under the control of another.

slide.d: An item of play equipment that children can climb up and then slide down again.
slide.e: The long, red slide was great fun for the kids.

slight.d: To give lesser weight or importance to.

slip.d: To cause to slip or slide off, or out of place.
slip.e: A horse slips his bridle; a dog slips his collar.

slope.d: To hold a rifle at a slope with forearm perpendicular to the body in front holding the butt, the rifle resting on the shoulder.
slope.e: The order was given to "slope arms".

solar.d: Of or pertaining to the sun; proceeding from the sun

soul.d: To beg on All Soul's Day.
soul.e: Fifty souls were lost when the ship sank.

specialist.d: An organism that is specialized for a particular environment.
specialist.e: The tree Lepidothamnus laxifolius is a high alpine specialist found in high-altitude bog communities and in scrub.

spiritual.d: Any spiritual function, office, or affair.
spiritual.e: He assigns supremacy to the pope in spirituals, and to the emperor in temporals. — Lowell.

split.d: Of something solid, to divide fully or partly along a more or less straight line.

sponsor.e: He was my sponsor when I applied to join the club.

stable.d: Of software: established to be relatively free of bugs, as opposed to a beta version.
stable.e: You should download the 1.9 version of that video editing software: it is the latest stable version. The newer beta version has some bugs.

stare.d: To influence in some way by looking fixedly.

status.d: A person’s condition, position or standing relative to that of others.
status.e: Superstition is highly correlated with economic status.

steady.d: Smooth and not bumpy or with obstructions.

steel.d: To edge, cover, or point with steel.
steel.e: India is one of the largest producers of steel in the world.

steep.d: A liquid used in a steeping process
steep.e: Corn steep has many industrial uses.

sticky.d: A sticky note, such as a post-it note.
sticky.e: Her desk is covered with yellow stickies.

stiff.d: An average person, usually male, of no particular distinction, skill, or education, often a working stiff or lucky stiff.
stiff.e: A Working Stiff's Manifesto: A Memoir of Thirty Jobs I Quit, Nine That Fired Me, and Three I Can't Remember was published in 2003.

stock.d: Of a type normally available for purchase/in stock.

stream.e: The soldier's wound was streaming blood.

stretch.d: To stretch the truth; to exaggerate.
stretch.e: a man apt to stretch in his report of facts

strict.d: Rigidly interpreted; exactly limited; confined; restricted.

strike.d: To hit forcibly and deliberately; to refuse to work as a form of organised protest.
strike.e: They struck off along the river.

struggle.d: To strive, to labour in difficulty, to fight (for or against), to contend.

submit.d: To yield or give way to another.

sum.d: A summary; the principal points or thoughts when viewed together; the amount; the substance; compendium.

surgery.d: A procedure involving major incisions to remove, repair, or replace a part of a body.

surround.d: To pass around; to travel about; to circumnavigate.

surrounding.d: To pass around; to travel about; to circumnavigate.

suspect.d: To imagine or suppose (something) to be true, or to exist, without proof.

swear.d: To take an oath, to promise.

sweep.e: Give the front steps a quick sweep to get rid of those fallen leaves.

sympathy.d: Tendency towards or approval of the aims of a movement.

tank.d: The amount held by a container; a tankful.

temporary.d: Not permanent; existing only for a period or periods of time.

therapy.e: Physical therapy helped him recover from the knee injury.

threat.d: To use threats; act or speak menacingly; threaten.

tough.d: Used to indicate lack of sympathy

transfer.d: To move or pass from one place, person or thing to another.

transform.d: To change greatly the appearance or form of.

transition.d: To bring through a transition; to change.
transition.e: The soldier was transitioned from a combat role to a strategic role.

treat.e: Only let my family live, I treat thee.

treatment.d: The process or manner of treating someone or something.

trend.d: To be the subject of a trend; to be currently popular, relevant or interesting.

trick.d: A single element of a magician's (or any variety entertainer's) act; a magic trick.

tropical.d: From or similar to a hot humid climate

truly.e: That is truly all I know.

trust.d: To place confidence in; to rely on, to confide, or have faith, in.

truth.d: True facts, genuine depiction or statements of reality.

tube.d: To supply with, or enclose in, a tube.
tube.e: She tubes lipstick in the cosmetics factory.

tune.d: The state or condition of being correctly tuned.

typically.d: In a typical or common manner.

ugly.d: Offensive to one's sensibilities or morality.

ultimately.e: Ultimately, he will have to make a decision before the end of the week.

unable.d: Not able; lacking a certain ability.

unconscious.e: My sudden fright was an unconscious response.

underwear.d: Clothes worn next to the skin, underneath outer clothing.

unemployment.d: The phenomenon of joblessness in an economy.
unemployment.e: Unemployment has been considered a cause of crime.

unexpected.e: The surprise visit from her old friend was unexpected.

unfair.e: It was unfair for the boss to give larger bonuses to his friends.

union.d: To combine sets using the union operation.

universe.d: Our universe, the sum of everything that exists in the cosmos, including time and space itself.

unknown.d: To undo the process of knowing, to lose knowledge of something.

upon.d: Being the target of an action.

upper.e: The upper floors of the building have a beautiful view.

upset.d: To be upset or knocked over.

upwards.d: To a higher figure or amount.

urban.e: Urban areas face challenges such as traffic congestion and pollution.

urge.d: To present in an urgent manner; to insist upon.

value.d: To estimate the value of; judge the worth of something.

venue.d: Sport venue: a stadium or similar building in which a sporting competition is held.

violence.d: Action which causes destruction, pain, or suffering.

vision.d: To present as in a vision.

vitamin.e: Oranges are a good source of vitamin C.

wage.d: To employ for wages; to hire.

weakness.e: In a small number of horses, muscle weakness may progress to paralysis.

wealth.d: A great amount; an abundance or plenty.

wealthy.d: Abundant in quality or quantity; profuse.

whisper.d: To mention privately and confidentially, or in a whisper.

witness.d: One who is called upon to witness an event or action, such as a wedding or the signing of a document.

wound.e: The police officer wounded the suspect during the fight that ensued.

absorb.d: To take in energy and convert it, as

abstract.e: He abstracted out the square root function.

accent.e: At this hotel, the accent is on luxury.

accidentally.d: In an accidental manner; by chance, unexpectedly.

accommodate.d: To provide with something desired, needed, or convenient.

accomplish.d: To execute fully; to fulfill; to complete successfully.
accomplish.e: to accomplish a design, an object, a promise

accountant.d: A reckoner, or someone who maintains financial matters for a person(s).

accuracy.d: The state of being accurate; being free from mistakes, this exemption arising from carefulness; exactness; correctness
accuracy.e: The value of testimony depends on its accuracy.

accurately.d: In an accurate manner; exactly; precisely; without error or defect.
accurately.e: She accurately translated the document from Hindi to English.

acid.d: Sour, sharp, or biting to the taste; tart; having the taste of vinegar.

activate.d: To render a molecule reactive, active, or effective in performing its function.
activate.e: Activate your account by clicking the link in the email.

activist.d: One who is conspicuously active in carrying out any occupational or professional functions.
activist.e: The environmental activist organised a beach cleanup drive.

acute.d: To give an acute sound to.

adaptation.d: A change that is made or undergone to suit a condition or environment.

addiction.d: The state of being addicted; devotion; inclination.
addiction.e: Social media addiction is a growing concern among teenagers.

adequate.d: Equal to or fulfilling some requirement.

adequately.d: Sufficient to satisfy a requirement or meet a need; sufficiently; satisfactorily.
adequately.e: The school is adequately equipped with computers and projectors.

adhere.d: To stick fast or cleave, as a glutinous substance does; to become joined or united.

adjacent.e: The picture is on the adjacent page.

adjust.e: Most immigrants adjust quickly to a new community.   She waited for her eyes to adjust to the darkness.

adjustment.d: The settling or balancing of a financial account
adjustment.e: The credit card company made an adjustment to my account to waive the late fee.

administer.d: To cause to ingest (a drug), either by openly offering or through deceit.
administer.e: We administered the medicine to our dog by mixing it in his food.

administrative.d: Of or relating to administering or administration.
administrative.e: She handles all the administrative tasks in the office.

administrator.d: One who is responsible for software installation, management, information and maintenance of a computer or network
administrator.e: The school administrator managed the admission process efficiently.

admission.d: The cost or fee associated with attendance or entry.
admission.e: There is no way he has seen that show, the admission is more than he makes in a week.

adolescent.d: Of, relating to, or at the age of adolescence; at the stage between being a child and an adult
adolescent.e: Adolescent students often face peer pressure in school.

adoption.d: The state of being adopted; the acceptance of a child of other parents as if he or she were one's own child.
adoption.e: A Chinese baby girl was given away for adoption.

adverse.d: Unfavorable; antagonistic in purpose or effect; hostile; actively opposing one's interests or wishes; contrary to one's welfare; acting against; working in an opposing direction.

advocate.e: I like trees, but I do not advocate living in them.

aesthetic.d: Beautiful or appealing to one's sense of beauty and/or art.
aesthetic.e: It works well enough, but the shabby exterior offends his aesthetic sensibilities.

affection.d: A feeling of love or strong attachment.
affection.e: I have a lot of affection for my little sister.

affordable.d: Able to be afforded; inexpensive or reasonably priced.
affordable.e: an affordable vacation in the local area

aftermath.d: That which happens after, that which follows, usually of strongly negative connotation in most contexts, implying a preceding catastrophe.
aftermath.e: In contrast to most projections of the aftermath of nuclear war, in this there is no rioting or looting.

aggression.d: The practice or habit of launching attacks.
aggression.e: Aggression should not be used to resolve conflicts.

agriculture.d: The art or science of cultivating the ground, including the harvesting of crops, and the rearing and management of livestock
agriculture.e: Agriculture is the main livelihood in rural India.

aide.d: An officer who acts as assistant to a more senior one; an aide-de-camp.
aide.e: The minister's aide arranged the official meetings.

aids.d: An aide-de-camp, so called by abbreviation.
aids.e: The incompetent general's brilliant aid often made priceless suggestions.

albeit.e: The hotel was small, albeit comfortable and clean.

align.d: To identify with or match the behaviour, thoughts, etc of another person.
align.e: Please align the text to the left margin.

alignment.d: The process of adjusting a mechanism such that its parts are aligned; the condition of having its parts so adjusted.
alignment.e: The alignment of the wheels needs to be checked.

alike.d: Having resemblance or similitude; similar; without difference.

allegation.d: An assertion, especially an accusation, not necessarily based on facts.
allegation.e: She put forth several allegations regarding her partner in hopes of discrediting his actions.

allege.d: To make a claim as justification or proof; to make an assertion without proof.
allege.e: The agency alleged my credit history had problems.

allegedly.d: According to someone's conspicuous impressions (which could be alleged ensuingly).

alliance.e: matrimonial alliances; an alliance between church and state, or between two countries

allocate.d: To distribute according to a plan, generally followed by the adposition to.

allocation.d: The process or procedure for allocating things, especially money or other resources.
allocation.e: The allocation of new permits is on a first-come, first-served basis.

allowance.d: To put upon a fixed allowance (especially of provisions and drink).
allowance.e: The captain was obliged to allowance his crew.

ally.d: A closely related species, usually within the same family.

alongside.d: Together with or at the same time.
alongside.e: The nurse worked alongside the doctor.

altogether.d: On the whole; with everything considered.

aluminium.d: Aircraft or other machinery made partially or wholly of aluminium.

amateur.d: Created, done, or populated by amateurs or non-professionals.

ambassador.d: A corporate representative, often the public face of the company.
ambassador.e: As front hall porter, you are an ambassador for the hotel.

ambulance.d: An emergency vehicle designed for transporting seriously ill or injured people to a hospital.
ambulance.e: The ambulance arrived within minutes of the call.

amend.d: To be healed, to be cured, to recover (from an illness).
amend.e: The parliament voted to amend the existing law.

amendment.d: An addition to and/or alteration to the Constitution.
amendment.e: The First Amendment guarantees freedom of religion, speech, press, assembly, and petition.

amid.d: In the middle of; in the center of; surrounded by.
amid.e: She remained calm amid the chaos around her.

amusing.d: To cause laughter or amusement; to be funny.

analogy.d: A relationship of resemblance or equivalence between two situations, people, or objects, especially when used as a basis for explanation or extrapolation.
analogy.e: The teacher used an analogy to explain the complex topic.

analyst.d: A financial analyst; a business analyst.
analyst.e: The financial analyst predicted a rise in the stock market.

ancestor.e: This fossil animal is regarded as the ancestor of the horse.

anchor.d: To cast anchor; to come to anchor.
anchor.e: Our ship (or the captain) anchored in the stream.

angel.d: A person having the qualities attributed to angels, such as purity or selflessness.
angel.e: Thanks for making me breakfast in bed, you little angel.

animation.d: The act of animating, or giving life or spirit.

annually.e: The festival is held annually in the month of January.

anonymous.d: Without any name acknowledged of a person responsible

The frost of etiquette, and pride of birth, began to give way before the genial blessings of this benign constellation, and the formal appellatives with which the three dignitaries had hitherto addressed each other, were now familiarly abbreviated into Tully, Bally, and Killie.

intensity.d: Can mean any of radiant intensity, luminous intensity or irradiance.

intensive.d: Related to the need to manage life-threatening conditions by means of sophisticated life support and monitoring.

intent.d: Firmly fixed or concentrated on something.

interfere.d: To get involved or involve oneself, causing disturbance.

interference.d: The illegal obstruction of an opponent in some ball games.
interference.e: They were glued to the TV, as the referee called out a fifteen yard penalty for interference.

interim.d: A transitional or temporary period between other events.

interior.d: Within any limits, enclosure, or substance; inside; internal; inner.

intermediate.d: To arrange, in the manner of a broker.

intervene.d: To become involved in a situation, so as to alter or prevent an action.
intervene.e: The police had to be called to intervene in the fight.

intervention.d: The action of intervening; interfering in some course of events.
intervention.e: Early medical intervention saved the patient's life.

intimate.d: Very close and familiar; having a warm personal relationship.
intimate.e: He intimated that we should leave before the argument escalated.

intriguing.d: To have clandestine or illicit intercourse.
intriguing.e: The mystery novel had an intriguing plot.

investigator.e: The investigator gathered evidence at the crime scene.

invisible.e: I went invisible so that my ex-girlfriend wouldn't send me instant messages.

invoke.d: To solicit, petition for, appeal to a favorable attitude.

involvement.d: The act of involving, or the state of being involved.

ironic.d: Contrary or opposite to what may be expected.

ironically.d: (evaluative) Used to draw attention to an ironic aspect of a situation being described.
ironically.e: Ironically, the automated checkout system ended up costing more in repairs than the workers it was meant to replace.

irony.d: Of or pertaining to the metal iron.
irony.e: The food had an irony taste to it.

irrelevant.d: Not related, not applicable, unimportant, not connected.

isolation.d: The state of being isolated, detached, or separated.

judicial.d: Of or relating to the court system or the judicial branch of government.

junction.d: The act of joining, or the state of being joined.

jurisdiction.d: The power, right, or authority to interpret and apply the law.
jurisdiction.e: The case falls under the jurisdiction of the high court.

justification.d: The alignment of text to the left margin (left justification), the right margin (right justification), or both margins (full justification).
justification.e: There is no justification for violence.

kidnap.d: The crime, or an instance, of kidnapping.

kidney.d: An organ in the body that filters the blood, producing urine.

kingdom.d: A realm, region, or conceptual space where something is dominant.

lad.d: A Jack the lad; a boyo.
lad.e: I think he reckons he's a bit of a lad.

landlord.d: A person who owns and rents land such as a house, apartment, or condo.

landmark.d: To officially designate a site or building as a landmark.

lap.d: To place or lay (one thing) so as to overlap another.

large-scale.d: Large in amount, scope or extent.

laser.e: The doctor used a laser to perform the eye surgery.

latter.d: Relating to or being the second of two items.

lawn.d: A piece of clothing made from lawn.
lawn.e: He mowed the lawn every weekend.

lawsuit.e: The lawyer advised his client against filing a lawsuit as it would take a lot of time and money to resolve.

layout.d: A structured arrangement of items within certain limits.
layout.e: The layout of the new office is spacious and modern.

leak.d: To allow fluid or gas to pass through an opening that should be sealed.

leap.d: To pass over by a leap or jump.

legacy.d: Something inherited from a predecessor or the past.

legendary.d: Of or pertaining to a legend or to legends.

legislation.d: Law which has been enacted by legislature or other governing body
legislation.e: The new legislation aims to protect consumer rights.

legislative.d: Making, or having the power to make, a law or laws; lawmaking

legislature.d: A governmental body with the power to make, amend and repeal laws.
legislature.e: The state legislature passed the education reform bill.

legitimate.d: Conforming to known principles, or established or accepted rules or standards; valid.

lengthy.d: Having length; long and overextended, especially in time rather than dimension.

lesbian.d: A homosexual woman, one who is mostly or exclusively sexually or romantically attracted to other women.

lesser.d: Small in amount or number, having few members.

lethal.e: The venom of some snakes can be lethal.

liable.d: Bound or obliged in law or equity; responsible; answerable.

liberal.e: He was liberal with his compliments.

liberation.d: The act of liberating or the state of being liberated.

liberty.d: The condition of being free from control or restrictions.

license.d: To give a formal (usually written) authorization.

lifelong.d: Extending for the entire duration of life.

likelihood.d: The probability of a specified outcome; the chance of something happening; probability; the state or degree of being probable.

limb.d: To remove the limbs from (an animal or tree).

line-up.d: Collectively, the members of a team.

linear.d: Made, or designed to be used, in a step-by-step, sequential manner.

linger.d: To stay or remain in a place or situation, especially as if unwilling to depart or not easily able to do so; to loiter.

listing.d: As listed or shown on a listing.

literacy.e: The literacy rate in India has improved significantly over the years.

liver.d: Of the colour of liver (dark brown, tinted with red and gray).

lobby.d: To attempt to influence (a public official or decision-maker) in favor of a specific opinion or cause.

log.d: To make, to add an entry (or more) in a log or logbook.

logic.d: The part of a system (usually electronic) that performs the boolean logic operations, short for logic gates or logic circuit.

long-standing.d: Having been done for long enough time to become convention.

long-time.d: Having endured for a long period of time.

loom.d: To appear indistinctly, eg. when seen on the horizon or through the murk.

loop.e: The program loops until the user presses a key.

loyalty.d: Faithfulness or devotion to some person, cause or nation.
loyalty.e: The company rewards employee loyalty with special bonuses.

machinery.d: The machines constituting a production apparatus, in a plant etc., collectively.
machinery.e: The factory installed new machinery to increase production.

magical.e: The fireworks created a magical atmosphere in that beautiful summer night.

magistrate.d: A judicial officer with limited authority to administer and enforce the law. A magistrate's court may have jurisdiction in civil or criminal cases, or both.
magistrate.e: The magistrate heard the case in the district court.

magnitude.d: A logarithmic scale of brightness defined so that a difference of 5 magnitudes is a factor of 100.

mainland.d: The principal island of a group.

mainstream.d: To educate (a disabled student) together with non-disabled students.

maintenance.d: Actions performed to keep some machine or system functioning or in service.

mandate.d: The authority to do something, as granted to a politician by the electorate.

mandatory.d: Of, being or relating to a mandate.

manipulation.d: The practice of manipulating or the state of being manipulated.

manuscript.d: Handwritten, or by extension manually typewritten, as opposed to being mechanically reproduced.

marginal.d: Of, relating to, or located at or near a margin or edge; also figurative usages of location and margin (edge).

marine.d: A soldier, normally a member of a marine corps, trained to serve on board or from a ship

marketplace.d: The space, actual or metaphorical, in which a market operates.

mature.d: Brought to a state of complete readiness.

maximize.d: To make as large as possible
maximize.e: The farmer used modern techniques to maximize crop yield.

meaningful.e: I think we made a meaningful contribution to this project today.

meantime.d: The time spent waiting for another event; time in between.

merchant.d: As a resident of a region, to buy goods from a non-resident and sell them to another non-resident.

methodology.d: A collection of methods, practices, procedures and rules used by those who work in some field.

midst.d: A place in the middle of something; may be used of a literal or metaphorical location.

naval.d: Of or relating to ships in general.

overwhelming.d: To engulf, surge over and submerge.
overwhelming.e: The dinghy was overwhelmed by the great wave.

pad.d: To increase the size of, especially by adding undesirable filler.

parameter.d: An input variable of a procedure definition, that gets an actual value (argument) at execution time (formal parameter).
parameter.e: Roughly, a tuple of arguments could be thought of as a vector, whereas a tuple of parameters could be thought of as a covector (i.e., linear functional). When a function is called, a parameter tuple becomes "bound" to an argument tuple, allowing the function instance itself to be computed to yield a return value. This would be roughly analogous to applying a covector to a vector (by taking their dot product (or, rather, matrix-product of row vector and column vector)) to obtain a scalar.

parental.d: A person fulfilling a parental role.
parental.e: Nowadays there are all kinds of potential parentals besides parents, grandparents, aunts and uncles, step-parents, in-laws, older siblings and cousins, and those in civil unions.

parish.d: To place (an area, or rarely a person) into one or more parishes.
parish.e: 1991, Melissa Bradley Kirkpatrick, Re-parishing the Countryside: Progressivism and Religious Interests in Rural Life Reform, 1908-1934

parliamentary.d: Of, relating to, or enacted by a parliament

partial.d: Describing a property that holds only when an algorithm terminates
partial.e: It's easy to prove partial correctness, but it's not obvious that it is also totally correct.

partially.d: To a partial degree or extent, incompletely.

passing.d: To change in state or status

passive.d: Being subjected to an action without producing a reaction.

pastor.d: To serve a congregation as pastor

pathway.d: A footpath or other path or track.

patron.e: This car park is for patrons only.

peasant.d: An uncouth, crude or ill-bred person.

peculiar.d: Out of the ordinary; odd; strange; unusual.

post-war.e: The post-war reconstruction transformed the country.

postpone.d: To delay or put off an event, appointment etc.
postpone.e: They decided to postpone the event due to heavy rain.

practitioner.d: A person who practices a profession or art, especially law or medicine.
practitioner.e: She is a medical practitioner at the local health centre.

preach.d: To teach or instruct by preaching; to inform by preaching.
preach.e: A learned local Muslim used to preach in the small mosque every Friday.

precedent.e: The court ruling set an important legal precedent.

precision.d: Used for exact or precise measurement.
precision.e: The craftsman worked with great precision on the jewellery.

predator.d: Any animal or other organism that hunts and kills other organisms (their prey), primarily for food.
predator.e: The tiger is a top predator in the Indian jungle.

predecessor.d: A model or type of machinery or device which precedes the current one. Usually used to describe an earlier, outdated model.
predecessor.e: The new principal continued the work of her predecessor.

predominantly.d: In a predominant manner. Most commonly or frequently by a large margin.
predominantly.e: The membership is predominantly elderly, 90% are over age 60.

pregnancy.e: Regular check-ups are important during pregnancy.

prejudice.d: An irrational hostile attitude, fear or hatred towards a particular group, race or religion.
prejudice.e: I am free of all prejudices. I hate everyone equally.

preliminary.d: In preparation for the main matter; initial, introductory, preparatory.
preliminary.e: These are just the preliminary results.

premier.d: To perform, display or exhibit for the first time.
premier.e: The composer invited all his friends when they premiered the movie he orchestrated, we got to see it before anyone but the crew.

premise.d: To state or assume something as a proposition to an argument.

premium.d: Superior in quality; higher in price or value.
premium.e: The insurance premium is due at the end of the month.

prescribe.d: To order (a drug or medical device) for use by a particular patient (under licensed authority).

prescription.d: The prescription medicine or intervention so prescribed.
prescription.e: The pharmacist gave her a bottle containing her prescription.

presently.e: He is presently working on a new research paper.

preservation.d: The act of preserving; care to preserve; act of keeping from destruction, decay or any ill.
preservation.e: The preservation of historical monuments is important.

preside.d: To act as president or chairperson.
preside.e: The chief justice will preside over the bench.

presidency.d: The office or role of president.
presidency.e: After many years as a party leader, she finally ascended to the presidency.

presidential.d: Pertaining to a president or presidency.
presidential.e: The presidential address was broadcast live on national television.

prestigious.e: She has a prestigious job with an international organization.

presumably.e: Presumably, he will attend the opening.

presume.d: With infinitive object: to be so presumptuous as (to do something) without proper authority or permission.
presume.e: I wouldn't presume to tell him how to do his job.

prevail.d: To succeed in persuading or inducing.
prevail.e: I prevailed on him to wait.

prevalence.d: The quality or condition of being prevalent; wide extension or spread.
prevalence.e: The prevalence of diabetes is increasing in urban India.

prevention.d: The act of preventing or hindering; obstruction of action, access, or approach; thwarting.

prey.d: A living thing that is eaten by another living thing.
prey.e: The rabbit was eaten by the coyote, so the rabbit is the coyote's prey.

privatization.d: The transfer of a company or organization from government to private ownership and control.
privatization.e: The privatization of certain services sparked a national debate.

privilege.d: To grant some particular right or exemption to; to invest with a peculiar right or immunity; to authorize

probe.e: If you probe further, you may discover different reasons.

problematic.d: Only affirming the possibility that a predicate be actualised.
problematic.e: The road conditions in rural areas can be problematic during monsoon.

proceedings.d: A measure or step taken in a course of business; a transaction
proceedings.e: a cautious or a violent proceeding

proceeds.d: To pass from one point, topic, or stage, to another.
proceeds.e: To proceed with a story or argument.

processing.d: To retrieve, store, classify, manipulate, transmit etc. (data, signals, etc.), especially using computer techniques.
processing.e: We have processed the data using our proven techniques, and have come to the following conclusions.

processor.d: A device which processes, which changes something (a computer processor, food processor, etc.).
processor.e: The food processor shredded the vegetables.

proclaim.e: The poet was proclaimed a national treasure.

productive.d: A type of set of natural numbers, related to mathematical logic.

productivity.d: The rate at which goods or services are produced by a standard population of workers
productivity.e: You can see the computer age everywhere but in the productivity statistics.

profitable.e: The startup became profitable within two years.

profound.d: Intellectually deep; entering far into subjects; reaching to the bottom of a matter, or of a branch of learning; thorough

projection.d: Something which projects, protrudes, juts out, sticks out, or stands out.
projection.e: The face of the cliff had many projections that were big enough for birds to nest on.

prominent.d: Standing out, or projecting; jutting; protuberant
prominent.e: The lawyer is a prominent figure in the legal community.

pronounced.d: To declare formally, officially or ceremoniously.
pronounced.e: I hereby pronounce you man and wife.

propaganda.d: A concerted set of messages aimed at influencing the opinions or behavior of large numbers of people.
propaganda.e: The students learned about propaganda techniques in history class.

proposition.d: A statement of religious doctrine; an article of faith; creed.
proposition.e: the propositions of Wyclif and Huss

prosecute.e: to prosecute a man for trespass, or for a riot

prosecution.d: The act of prosecuting a scheme or endeavor.
prosecution.e: The prosecution of the war fell to Winston Churchill.

prosecutor.d: A person, as a complainant, victim, or chief witness, who institutes prosecution in a criminal proceeding.
prosecutor.e: The prosecutor got the witness to admit he was lying.

prospective.d: Likely or expected to happen or become.
prospective.e: Prospective students are those who have already applied to the university, but have yet to be admitted.

prosperity.d: The condition of being prosperous, of having good fortune
prosperity.e: Education is the key to prosperity and progress.

protective.d: Serving, intended or wishing to protect
protective.e: Mother bears are famously protective of their cubs.

protocol.e: The hospital follows strict safety protocols.

province.d: An administrative subdivision of certain countries, including Canada and China.
province.e: Each province has its own distinct culture and traditions.

provincial.d: Of or pertaining to a province.

provision.d: A liability or contra account to recognise likely future adverse events associated with current transactions.
provision.e: We increased our provision for bad debts on credit sales going into the recession.

provoke.d: To cause someone to become annoyed or angry.
provoke.e: Don't provoke the dog; it may try to bite you.

psychiatric.e: The psychiatric ward provides care for patients with mental health issues.

pulse.d: To beat, to throb, to flash.
pulse.e: In the dead of night, all was still but the pulsing light.

pump.d: To use a pump to move (liquid or gas).
pump.e: I've pumped over 1000 gallons of water in the last ten minutes.

punch.e: If she punches me, I'm gonna break her nose.

query.d: To send out a query letter.
query.e: The teacher answered the student’s query concerning biosynthesis.

quest.d: To seek or pursue a goal; to undertake a mission or job.
quest.e: to rove in quest of game, of a lost child, of property, etc.

quota.d: A prescribed number or percentage that may serve as, for example, a maximum, a minimum, or a goal.
quota.e: The university has a reserved quota for economically weaker students.

radar.d: To scan with radar, or as if with radar.
radar.e: His sensitive radar for hidden alliances keeps him out of trouble.

radical.d: Favoring fundamental change, or change at the root cause of a matter.

rage.e: Miniskirts were all the rage back then.

raid.d: To engage in a raid against.
raid.e: The police raided the gambling den.

rally.d: A public gathering or mass meeting that is not mainly a protest and is organized to inspire enthusiasm for a cause.

ranking.e: Their defense ranked third in the league.

ratio.d: A number representing a comparison between two named things.
ratio.e: The schoolchildren ganged up to ratio the school catering over its shamefully undemocratic inclusion of vegetables in meals.

rational.d: A rational number: a number that can be expressed as the quotient of two integers.
rational.e: The quotient of two rationals is again a rational.

ray.d: To emit something as if in rays.
ray.e: I saw a ray of light through the clouds.

readily.d: Without unwillingness or hesitation; showing readiness.
readily.e: The suspect readily answered all questions the police officers asked him.

realization.d: The result of an artistic effort.
realization.e: The painter's realization of her concept was breathtaking.

realm.d: An abstract sphere of influence, real or imagined.
realm.e: She is an expert in the realm of international law.

rear.d: To rise up on the hind legs
rear.e: The horse was shocked, and thus reared.

reasoning.d: To persuade by reasoning or argument.
reasoning.e: to reason one into a belief; to reason one out of his plan

reassure.d: To assure anew; to restore confidence to; to free from fear or self-doubt.
reassure.e: The boy's mother reassured him that there was no monster hiding under the bed.

rebel.d: To resist or become defiant toward an authority.
rebel.e: The rebels surrendered their weapons during the peace talks.

rebellion.d: Armed resistance to an established government or ruler.
rebellion.e: The government is doing its best to stop rebellion in the country.

recipient.d: An individual receiving donor organs or tissues.
recipient.e: My e-mail never reached the intended recipient.

reconstruction.d: The act of restoring something to an earlier state.
reconstruction.e: The reconstruction of the medieval bridge began last year.

referendum.d: An action, choice, etc., which is perceived as passing judgment on another matter.
referendum.e: My father is taking my decision on whether to go to university as a referendum on his performance as a parent, and it's very stressful.

reflection.d: Something, such as an image, that is reflected.
reflection.e: The dog barked at his own reflection in the mirror.

reform.d: To form again or in a new configuration.
reform.e: The regiment reformed after surviving the first attack.

refuge.d: To return to a place of shelter.
refuge.e: The hikers took refuge in a hut during the storm.

refusal.d: Depth or point at which well or borehole drilling cannot continue.
refusal.e: Her refusal to compromise earned her respect.

regain.d: To get back; to recover possession of.
regain.e: The athlete trained hard to regain his fitness after the injury.

regardless.d: Without attention to warnings or indications of bad consequences.
regardless.e: Tatiana knew the meat had passed its expiry date, but she ate it regardless.

regime.e: Heaven will eliminate the tyrannical regimes.

regulator.d: A device that controls or limits something.
regulator.e: The voltage regulator stopped working and the resulting overload destroyed the device.

regulatory.e: The regulatory body monitors food safety standards.

rehabilitation.d: The process of rehabilitating somebody or something.
rehabilitation.e: The rehabilitation centre helps patients recover from addiction.

reign.d: To exercise sovereign power, to rule as a monarch.
reign.e: He reigned in an autocratic manner.

rejection.e: He handled the rejection with grace and dignity.

relevance.d: The property or state of being relevant or pertinent.
relevance.e: The relevance of the topic to current events made it interesting.

reliability.d: The quality of being reliable, dependable or trustworthy.
reliability.e: The reliability of the bus service has improved recently.

reluctant.d: Not wanting to take some action; unwilling.
reluctant.e: She was reluctant to lend him the money

remainder.d: To mark or declare items left unsold as subject to reduction in price.
remainder.e: The bookstore remaindered the unsold copies of that book at the end of summer at a reduced price.

remains.d: To continue in a state of being.
remains.e: The light remained red for two full minutes.

remedy.d: To provide or serve as a remedy for.
remedy.e: Turmeric milk is a traditional remedy for colds in India.

reminder.e: He left a note as a reminder to get groceries.

removal.d: The process of moving, or the fact of being removed.
removal.e: The removal of the old furniture made room for the new bookshelf.

render.d: To interpret, give an interpretation or rendition of.
render.e: The pianist rendered the Beethoven sonata beautifully.

renew.d: To extend a period of loan, especially a library book that is due to be returned.
renew.e: I'd like to renew these three books.  Did you know that you can renew online?

renowned.e: The movie “Three Stars” is about world-renowned chefs.

rental.d: The payment made to rent something.
rental.e: It's just a rental, so I don't need to take very good care of it.

replacement.e: The replacement of that broken light-bulb will have to wait until I can buy a new one.

reportedly.d: According to reports or rumors; supposedly.
reportedly.e: The temple is reportedly over a thousand years old.

representation.d: The ability to elect a representative to speak on one's behalf in government; the role of this representative in government.
representation.e: The lack of representation in the British parliament was one of the main factors behind the American Revolution.

reproduce.d: To produce an image or copy of.
reproduce.e: The artist can reproduce the painting in exact detail.

reproduction.e: Unauthorized reproduction of this article is prohibited.

republic.d: One of the subdivisions constituting Russia. See oblast.
republic.e: The Republic of Udmurtia is west of the Permian Oblast.

resemble.d: To be like or similar to (something); to represent as similar.

reside.d: To dwell permanently or for a considerable time; to have a settled abode for a time; to remain for a long time.
reside.e: Many IT professionals reside in Bengaluru.

residence.d: A building used as a home.
residence.e: The Prime Minister's official residence is in New Delhi.

residential.d: Of or pertaining to a place of personal residence or to a location for such places.
residential.e: They live in a residential neighborhood.

residue.d: Whatever remains after something else has been removed.
residue.e: Wash the vegetables thoroughly to remove pesticide residue.

resignation.d: A written or oral declaration that one resigns.
resignation.e: She submitted her resignation after ten years of service.

resistance.d: The act of resisting, or the capacity to resist.
resistance.e: the resistance of bacteria to certain antibiotics

respective.d: Relating to particular persons or things, each to each; particular; own.
respective.e: They returned to their respective places of abode.

respectively.d: In a relative manner; often used when comparing lists, where the term denotes that the items in the lists correspond to each other in the order they are given.
respectively.e: Serena Williams and Roger Federer won the women's and men's singles titles, respectively, at the 2010 Australian Open.

restoration.d: The return of a former monarchy or monarch to power, usually after having been forced to step down.
restoration.e: The restoration of the House of Stuart took place a few years after the death of Cromwell.

restraint.d: Something that restrains, ties, fastens or secures
restraint.e: Make sure all the restraints are tight.

resume.d: To start (something) again that has been stopped or paused from the point at which it was stopped or paused; continue, carry on.
resume.e: We will resume this discussion tomorrow at nine.

retreat.d: To withdraw from a position, go back.

retrieve.d: To regain or get back something.
retrieve.e: to retrieve one's character or independence; to retrieve a thrown ball

revelation.d: The act of revealing or disclosing.
revelation.e: The revelation of the new product excited the audience.

revenge.d: To take revenge for (a particular harmful action) or on behalf of (its victim); to avenge.
revenge.e: Arsenal revenged its loss to Manchester United last time with a 5–0 drubbing this time.

reverse.d: To turn something around so that it faces the opposite direction or runs in the opposite sequence.
reverse.e: to reverse a portion of video footage

revival.d: Renewed prevalence of something, as a practice or a fashion.

revive.d: To recover from a state of oblivion, obscurity, neglect, or depression.
revive.e: Classical learning revived in the fifteenth century.

revolutionary.d: Of or pertaining to a revolution in government; tending to, or promoting, revolution

rhetoric.d: Part of or similar to rhetoric, the use of language as a means to persuade.
rhetoric.e: A rhetorical question is one used merely to make a point, with no response expected.

rifle.d: To quickly search through many items (such as papers, the contents of a drawer, a pile of clothing). (See also rifflehttp//verbmall.blogspot.com/2008/05/riffle-or-rifle.html)
rifle.e: She made a mess when she rifled through the stack of papers, looking for the title document.

riot.d: To create or take part in a riot; to raise an uproar or sedition.
riot.e: The nuclear protesters rioted outside the military base.

rip.d: To tear apart; to rapidly become two parts.
rip.e: My shirt ripped when it was caught on a bramble.

ritual.d: Related to a rite or repeated set of actions.
ritual.e: They performed the ritual lighting of the candles.

robust.e: A robust wall was put up.

rock.d: To play, perform, or enjoy rock music, especially with a lot of skill or energy.

rod.d: A unit of area equal to a square rod, 30 1/4 square yards or 1/160 acre.
rod.e: The house had a small yard of about six rods in size.

rotate.e: He rotated in his chair to face me.

rotation.d: The act of turning around a centre or an axis.
rotation.e: The earth's rotation about its axis is responsible for its being slightly oblate rather than a sphere.

ruling.d: That rules; predominant; chief; reigning; controlling.

rumour.d: A statement or claim of questionable accuracy, from no known reliable source, usually spread by word of mouth.
rumour.e: There's a rumor going round that he's going to get married.

sack.d: To put in a sack or sacks.

sacred.d: Characterized by solemn religious ceremony or religious use, especially, in a positive sense; consecrated, made holy.

sacrifice.d: To offer (something) as a gift to a deity.
sacrifice.e: Venison has many advantages over meat from factory farms, although it still requires a hunter to sacrifice the life of a deer.

saint.d: To canonize, to formally recognize someone as a saint.
saint.e: Many wish to see Pope John Paul II sainted immediately.

sake.d: The benefit or regard of someone or something

sanction.d: To penalize (a State etc.) with sanctions.
sanction.e: The whalers had been operating in the contested waters off the island with sanction from the Japanese government.

scattered.d: To distribute loosely as by sprinkling.
scattered.e: Her ashes were scattered at the top of a waterfall.

sceptical.e: I can see why people are so skeptical [sic] about him, but I think he's on to something here. (regarded by organizations such as the BBC as an error)

scope.e: The entomologist explained that he could not tell what species of springtail we were looking at without scoping it.

screw.d: To connect or assemble pieces using a screw.
screw.e: the skeleton screw (Caprella);  the sand screw

scrutiny.d: Intense study of someone or something.
scrutiny.e: The government project came under public scrutiny.

seal.d: To place in a sealed container.
seal.e: I've sealed the documents in this envelope.

secular.d: Not bound by the vows of a monastic order.

seemingly.d: In a seemly manner; decorously; with propriety.
seemingly.e: The seemingly simple puzzle turned out to be very difficult.

segment.d: To divide into segments or sections.

seize.d: To bind or lock in position immovably; see also seize up.
seize.e: Rust caused the engine to seize, never to run again.

selective.d: Of or relating to natural selection.

senator.d: A member, normally elected, in the house or chamber of a legislature called a senate. The legislatures of the United States and Canada have senators.
senator.e: The senator spoke passionately about education reform.

sensation.d: A widespread reaction of interest or excitement.
sensation.e: The cool breeze was a pleasant sensation on the hot day.

sensitivity.d: The ability of an organism or organ to respond to external stimuli.
sensitivity.e: The teacher handled the issue with great sensitivity.

sentiment.d: A general thought, feeling, or sense.
sentiment.e: The sentiment emerged that we were acting too soon.

separation.d: The act of disuniting two or more things, or the condition of being separated.
separation.e: The separation of duties ensures fairness in the organisation.

serial.d: A serial number, esp. one required to activate software.
serial.e: Go to these sites for serials, cracks and keygens.

set-up.d: The fashion in which something is organized or arranged.
set-up.e: The classroom setup was simple and efficient.

settlement.e: The legal settlement was reached out of court.

severely.e: The crops were severely damaged by the hailstorm.

sexuality.d: The quality of being sexual; that which is characterized or distinguished by sex.
sexuality.e: The biology class discussed human sexuality in a scientific context.

sexy.d: That can sexually attract or arouse.

shaped.d: To give something a shape and definition.
shaped.e: Shape the dough into a pretzel.   For my art project, I plan to shape my clay lump into a bowl.

shareholder.d: One who owns shares of stock in a corporation.
shareholder.e: Shareholders are the real owners of a publicly traded business, but management runs it.

shatter.e: to break a glass into shatters

shed.e: A metal comb shed her golden hair.

sheer.e: Use sheers to maximize natural light.

shipping.d: To send (a parcel or container) to a recipient (by any means of transport).

shocking.d: To cause to be emotionally shocked, to cause (someone) to feel surprised and upset.

shore.d: Land adjoining a non-flowing body of water, such as an ocean, lake or pond.
shore.e: lake shore;  bay shore;  gulf shore;  island shore;  mainland shore;  river shore;  estuary shore;  pond shore;  sandy shore;  rocky shore

short-term.d: Of or pertaining to the near or immediate future.
short-term.e: This short-term plan deals with the next few days.

shortage.d: A lack or deficiency; an insufficient amount.
shortage.e: The water shortage affected many villages during the summer.

shortly.e: Ideas are generally expressed more shortly in verse than in prose

shrug.d: To raise (the shoulders) to express uncertainty, lack of concern, (formerly) dread, etc.
shrug.e: I asked him for an answer and he just shrugged.

sibling.d: A person who shares a parent; one's brother or sister who one shares a parent with.
sibling.e: None of my siblings are married yet.

sigh.d: To experience an emotion associated with sighing.
sigh.e: He silently sighed for his lost youth.

signature.e: Rabbit in mustard sauce is my signature dish.

significance.d: The extent to which something matters; importance
significance.e: As a juror your opinion is of great significance for the outcome of the trial.

simulate.d: To model, replicate, duplicate the behavior, appearance or properties of.
simulate.e: We will use a smoke machine to simulate the fog you will actually encounter.

simulation.d: Something that simulates a system or environment in order to predict actual behaviour.
simulation.e: The most reliable simulation predicts that the hurricane will turn north.

simultaneously.e: The event was broadcast simultaneously on television and radio.

sin.d: A violation of God's will or religious law.
sin.e: As a Christian, I think this is a sin against God.

situated.d: To place on or into a physical location.
situated.e: The statue is situated in a corner hardly visible to the public, except through a window from an outside maintenance area situated behind the building.

sketch.d: To make a brief, basic drawing.
sketch.e: I usually sketch with a pen rather than a pencil.

skilled.d: To spend acquired points in exchange for skills.
skilled.e: India has a large pool of skilled IT professionals.

skip.d: To move by hopping on alternate feet.
skip.e: She will skip from one end of the sidewalk to the other.

skull.d: To hit in the head with a fist, a weapon, or a thrown object.
skull.e: The divers encountered a huge school of mackerel.

slam.d: To shut with sudden force so as to produce a shock and noise.

slap.e: She slapped him in response to the insult.

slash.d: The loose woody debris remaining from a slash, (particularly forestry) the trimmings left while preparing felled trees for removal.
slash.e: Slash generated during logging may constitute a fire hazard.

slavery.d: An institution or social practice of owning human beings as property, especially for use as forced laborers.
slavery.e: The museum exhibit highlighted the history of slavery.

slogan.d: A catch phrase associated with the product or service being advertised.
slogan.e: The campaign slogan inspired millions of people.

slot.d: A slot machine designed for gambling.
slot.e: I walked past the poker tables and went straight to the slots.

smash.d: To be destroyed by being smashed.
smash.e: The crockery smashed as it hit the floor.

snap.d: To cause something to emit a snapping sound.

so-called.d: So named; commonly called by such a name, often with a connotation that the item is not worthy of that name.
so-called.e: These so-called "assistants" are making our jobs harder, not easier!

soak.d: To be saturated with liquid by being immersed in it.
soak.e: I'm going to soak in the bath for a couple of hours.

soar.d: To rise, especially rapidly or unusually high.
soar.e: The pump prices soared into new heights as the strike continued.

socialist.d: Of, promoting, practicing, or characteristic of socialism.
socialist.e: The party follows socialist principles of equal distribution.

sole.d: To put a sole on (a shoe or boot)
sole.e: She is the sole breadwinner in her family.

solely.e: The new chef was solely responsible for attending the grill.

solicitor.d: In parts of the U.S., the chief legal officer of a city, town or other jurisdiction.
solicitor.e: The solicitor advised his client on the legal matter.

solidarity.d: Willingness to give psychological and/or material support when another person is in a difficult position or needs affection.
solidarity.e: Only the solidarity provided by her siblings allowed Margaret to cope with her mother's harrowing death.

solo.d: Of, or relating to, a musical solo.
solo.e: She gave a solo performance at the music festival.

somehow.d: In one way or another; in a way not yet known or explained; by some means
somehow.e: This problem has to be tackled somehow.

sometime.d: Former, erstwhile; at some previous time.

sophisticated.d: To make less natural or innocent.
sophisticated.e: The telescope is a sophisticated piece of scientific equipment.

sovereignty.d: (of a polity) The state of making laws and controlling resources without the coercion of other nations.
sovereignty.e: Every nation has the right to protect its sovereignty.

spam.d: To send spam (i.e. unsolicited electronic messages.)

span.d: To measure by the span of the hand with the fingers extended, or with the fingers encompassing the object.
span.e: to span a space or distance; to span a cylinder

spare.d: Held in reserve, to be used in an emergency.
spare.e: a spare anchor; a spare bed or room

spark.d: To give off a spark or sparks.
spark.e: The speech sparked a lively debate among the students.

specialize.d: To make distinct or separate, particularly:
specialize.e: The hospital specializes in cardiac surgery.

specialized.d: To make distinct or separate, particularly:
specialized.e: She received specialized training in data science.

specification.d: An explicit set of requirements to be satisfied by a material, product, or service.
specification.e: The building was constructed according to strict specifications.

specify.d: To state explicitly, or in detail, or as a condition.
specify.e: Please specify the size and colour of the shirt you want.

specimen.d: An individual instance that represents a class; an example.
specimen.e: early specimens of the art of Picasso

spectacle.d: An exciting or extraordinary scene, exhibition, performance etc.
spectacle.e: The horse race was a thrilling spectacle.

spectacular.d: Amazing or worthy of special notice.

spectator.d: One who watches an event; especially, one held outdoors.
spectator.e: The cheering spectators watched the fireworks.

spectrum.d: Specifically, a range of colours representing light (electromagnetic radiation) of contiguous frequencies; hence electromagnetic spectrum, visible spectrum, ultraviolet spectrum, etc.
spectrum.e: The course covers a wide spectrum of topics.

speculate.d: To anticipate which branch of code will be chosen and execute it in advance.
speculate.e: Analysts speculate that the economy will grow by seven per cent.

speculation.d: A conclusion to which the mind comes by speculating; mere theory; notion; conjecture.
speculation.e: There is much speculation about the new government policy.

sphere.d: To place in a sphere, or among the spheres; to ensphere.
sphere.e: The globe is shaped like a sphere.

spice.d: To add spice or spices to; season.
spice.e: India is famous for its spices like cardamom, turmeric, and pepper.

spin.d: To make yarn by twisting and winding fibers together.

taxpayer.e: 1945: I got into the bus full of taxpayers who were giving some money to a taxpayer who had on his taxpayer's stomach a little box which allowed the other taxpayers to continue their taxpayers' journeys. I noticed in this bus a taxpayer with a long taxpayer's neck and whose taxpayer's head bore a taxpayer's felt hat encircled by a plait the like of which no taxpayer ever wore before. Suddenly the said taxpayer peremptorily addressed a nearby taxpayer, complaining bitterly that he was purposely treading on his taxpayer's toes every time other taxpayers got on or off the taxpayers' bus. Then the angry taxpayer went and sat down in a seat for taxpayers which another taxpayer had just vacated. — Raymond Queneau, 'from Polyptotes' in Exercises in Style, 1945 (Eng. 1958)

layer).d: A sheet, coat, or thickness of material covering a surface.
